grid_desc: [mô tả, realm, stage, factorial, input, process, output]

Net worth is zero or negative. Lacks investment capacity. Lacks financial literacy, relies on welfare or a low fixed salary. The biggest opportunity cost is missing out on an undervalued asset while capital is tied up elsewhere. Focus on determining the intrinsic value of a business based on cash flow and real assets, buying when the market price is significantly below its intrinsic value to create a sustainable margin of safety.

Start with minimal capital to experience the market. Know how to buy and sell commodities, and use basic stock/crypto trading. Entry speed is crucial at the beginning of a long-term holding cycle (a good entry determines the holding mindset). Long-term strategies are based on belief in economic growth, eliminating short-term disturbances to optimize taxes and transaction costs over many years.

Individual proprietary trading. Knows basic chart reading, prone to FOMO (fear of missing out). Signal accuracy is demonstrated through Japanese candlestick patterns. Use candlestick patterns like Hammer, Engulfing, or Doji to understand the psychology of buyers and sellers at key price reversal points.

Manage capital at a low to medium level. Begin to develop skills. Have your own trading system and understand low-level risk management. Diversification is not just about buying many stocks, but about dividing capital into fixed proportions to manage overall risk. Determine trading volume based on a fixed percentage of total capital, ensuring the account is not wiped out during a losing streak.

Start to build a basic investment portfolio. Master technical and fundamental analysis. Optimize compound interest by continuously targeting price regressions to the average. Based on the assumption that prices always tend to return to historical averages after being excessively stretched by temporary fluctuations.

Be able to handle financial leverage. Manage a multi-asset portfolio and know how to use derivatives. Use historical data to identify support/resistance zones – the "sensitive" points of the past. Identify past buy/sell order concentration zones to find entry points when the price touches or breaks through these psychological thresholds.
input_2: High Low Avg / Support and Resistance

grid_desc: Start approaching trust funds. Leverage tax and corporate structure. Win rates are often verified through the crossover of indicators (e.g., MA20/MA50). Use the crossover of moving averages (MA) or momentum indicators like MACD to generate highly disciplined buy/sell signals.

Become more influential within a small group. Achieve complete financial independence and have your own support team. Trading volume is the "soul" of tape reading. Monitor the tape and real-time trading volume to identify the entry of large institutions before price movements occur.

Capable of manipulating small-cap stocks. Acquiring businesses, manipulating the prices of mid-cap stocks. Allocating assets based on identifying large-scale chart patterns across different asset classes. Trading based on geometric structures such as Head and Shoulders, Pennant, or Double Bottom to predict the continuation or reversal of a trend.

Private Banking is a preferred target. Accessing "closed-loop" deals (Private Equity). Rotating capital to always seek sectors with the highest dividend yields. Shifting capital into sectors benefiting from economic cycles (such as energy during inflation, technology during periods of low interest rates).

Capable of leading a trend. Building a private banking system or financial institution. Only using leverage when there is consistent confirmation across multiple timeframes (minimizing the risk of account liquidation). Analyzing trends on larger timeframes (Daily/Weekly) and finding optimal entry points on smaller timeframes (H1/M15) to increase the Reward/Risk ratio.

International scope. Lobbying at the national level, changing industry rules. Isolating risk by testing and optimizing parameters to eliminate confounding variables. Using historical data to simulate strategies, thereby adjusting parameters to achieve optimal performance before execution with real capital.

Influencing the economy of a region/industry. Acquiring transnational competitors. Network effects are at the core of global macroeconomic capital flows. Betting on fluctuations in interest rates, exchange rates, and geopolitics based on macroeconomic reports from major countries.

Capable of influencing economic policy. Intervening in national macroeconomic policies and exchange rates. Market makers need a constantly "live" system to provide continuous liquidity. Providing market liquidity by placing buy and sell orders simultaneously, profiting from the Bid-Ask Spread.

Manipulating exchange rates and national indices. Instigating financial crises for profit. Access speed directly affects whether stop-loss/take-profit orders are executed at the correct price. Setting automatic exit thresholds based on volatility (ATR) or technical levels to protect profits and limit risk.

Capital from the national budget. Using state capital to acquire strategic global assets. Superior returns generated from algorithmic smart order execution (reducing slippage). Using robots to split large orders into many smaller orders to minimize price impact and conceal the fund's trading intentions.

The scale of historical financial empires. Building hereditary family empires (Old Money). SMC focuses on finding deep liquidity zones where "big money" places orders. The trading system focuses on market structure (BOS, CHOCH) and liquidity zones to align with central banks.

It dominates global money flows and controls voting at most companies in the S&P 500. Scalpers need top-of-the-line hardware (mouse, keyboard, monitor) and connectivity due to the extremely high trading frequency. They execute hundreds of orders daily with holding times measured in seconds or minutes to accumulate large profits from micro-fluctuations.

Regulating large-scale market liquidity. Secretly manipulating the money supply and interest rates. Liquidity ratios are a prerequisite for executing balanced hedging orders. Using options and underlying assets to establish portfolios insensitive to price movements, profiting only from volatility or time.

Nearly holding the world's debt. Managing global public debt and international bailout packages. Utilizing ultra-fast technological infrastructure to exploit arbitrage opportunities in an instant imperceptible to the human eye.

The financial power of an entire continent. Dominating the continent's natural resources and energy. Quantitative strategy testing is a form of "auditing" compliance with mathematical rules. Using statistical methods (such as Monte Carlo) to assess the sustainability of strategies and systemic risk tolerance.

The designer of the monetary/standard system. Establishing new monetary standards (such as the gold, fiat, or crypto standard). The system's solvency is maintained through index rebalancing. Exploiting price volatility as ETFs are forced to buy or sell stocks to match the new weightings of indices like the S&P 500.

Determining the prosperity of an era. Erasing or creating industrial revolutions. Trading news is like having "early access" to dramatic market movements. Taking advantage of volatility following news events such as earnings reports, mergers and acquisitions (M&A), or sudden policy changes.

Owning assets exceeding the total world GDP. Owning land and property rights on a global scale. A strategy that doesn't bet on price direction but on market volatility through Straddle or Strangle combinations.

Not appearing on Forbes lists. Operating outside the control of any government or law. Portfolio rebalancing is a proactive way to pull accounts out of maximum decline. Regularly adjust asset allocation to target levels to sell overheated assets and buy more undervalued assets.

Control all resources and lives. Control humanity's survival system (food, water, air). Fundamental analysis helps understand why organizations leave footprints at certain price levels. Identify large volume accumulation and distribution zones through Dark Pool data and block trades.

Extend your control over extraterrestrial resources. Exploit and trade resources between planets. Mergers often create opportunities for Long/Short strategies between related companies. Buy the strongest stocks in an industry and simultaneously short-sell the weakest to eliminate overall market risk (Beta).

The ability to print/create value at will. Transform nothing into value, eliminating the concept of capital scarcity. Implement multi-layered asset management protocols to protect capital against systemic risks or cyberattacks (especially in Crypto/Digital Assets).

The ultimate point of all material value. The unity of will and matter. Possession of all valuable entities. Gradually taking profits as prices reach targets to lock in gains while retaining a portion of the position to pursue larger trends.

Assets that cannot be measured by specific numbers. Transcending the concept of parity. Monitoring virtual liquidity to uncover hidden orders (Iceberg orders) from large players. Analyzing spoofing and hidden orders in the order book to distinguish between real demand and market manipulation.

Transcending all concepts of mathematics and finance. Existing in a financial multiverse where value is constant. Today's automated bots are linked to the self-optimizing capabilities of AI/ML. Using Reinforcement Learning algorithms to allow the system to adjust its strategy based on changing real-world market conditions.

A state beyond value. Priceless. A state undefined by human thought. When there are no longer pressures related to fees and technical issues (no-code), trading psychology reaches its freest state. This is the highest level of professional investing, where discipline and composure become the ultimate operating system, eliminating all emotional barriers.
